heading: About Henry Perkins
intro: I have worked on technical projects that stay close to real customer needs: an AI meeting transcript summarizer, a support-focused FAQ chatbot prototype, WordPress and WooCommerce client builds, and documentation systems that make troubleshooting easier to hand off.

What ties those projects together is the same throughline in my support, community, and consulting background: I like taking messy information and turning it into clear workflows, calmer operations, and practical tools that non-specialists can actually use.

I am especially interested in scaling up my AI and WordPress skills because they fit the work I already do best. AI helps me prototype better summaries, triage, and self-service experiences; WordPress remains one of the fastest ways to ship useful business outcomes. Growing in both areas is part of how I am working toward stable, living-wage technical work rooted in customer empathy and execution.

valueCards.title: Customer Empathy
valueCards.description: I treat every support request like a human conversation first, then solve the technical root cause.

valueCards.title: Clarity
valueCards.description: Clear notes, reproducible debugging steps, and clean handoffs keep projects moving without surprises.

valueCards.title: Execution
valueCards.description: I prioritize practical delivery so teams can see progress quickly and build confidence with each release.
